##NAMES=Gajardoite
##RRUFFID=R250058
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KCa_0.5_As^3+^_4_O_6_Cl_2_·5H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Rosette-like subparallel intergrowths of transparent hexagonal plates associated with native arsenic, arsenolite, chongite, talmessite and torrecillasite. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2016)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250058
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(K_0.77_Ca_0.71_Na_0.05_Mg_0.05_)As_4_O_11_Cl_1.96_H_9.62_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.2592 b: 5.2592 c: 15.971 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 382.56 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
